qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Zip path traversal in safe_extract allows arbitrary file/directory creation outside extraction target
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the exact same vulnerable code and root cause as the advisory: the misuse of zip.extract() with os.path.join(target_directory, file) in guarddog/utils/archives.py line 77, which causes the filename to be appended twice and breaks zipfile's built-in path traversal sanitization. Both the advisory and finding describe the same CWE-22 path traversal vulnerability in the same function, file, and line of code.

selected_finding.title: Zip path traversal in safe_extract allows arbitrary file/directory creation outside extraction target

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding perfectly matches the advisory-described vulnerability: both identify the same path traversal vulnerability in guarddog/utils/archives.py line 77, where zip.extract(file, path=os.path.join(target_directory, file)) causes the filename to be appended twice, breaking zipfile's built-in path traversal sanitization. However, PoC execution failed due to network connectivity issues preventing the cloning of the vulnerable repository from GitHub. Without runtime artifacts demonstrating exploit success or bug activation, this qualifies as Tier3.
evidence: Finding identifies the exact vulnerable code location: guarddog/utils/archives.py:77
Finding describes the same root cause: misuse of zip.extract() path parameter causing duplicate filename append
Finding describes the same CWE-22 path traversal vulnerability class
Finding describes the same attack vector: malicious PyPI packages with path traversal filenames
Finding describes the same impact boundary violation: files created outside intended extraction directory
Advisory CVE-2026-22871 references the same GuardDog safe_extract vulnerability
Original PoC report confirms reproduction success with marker file escaping target directory
blockers_to_higher_tier: PoC execution failed: Network connectivity prevented cloning DataDog/guarddog repository from GitHub
No runtime artifacts available to satisfy exploit_success_predicate or bug_activation_predicate
Cannot demonstrate Tier1 end-to-end exploit success without successful PoC execution
Cannot demonstrate Tier2 bug activation without runtime artifacts showing vulnerable logic triggered

poc_executions.observed_effect: PoC execution failed due to network connectivity issue. The reproduction script requires cloning the DataDog/guarddog repository from GitHub, which failed with 'Failed to connect to github.com port 443 after 75002 ms: Couldn't connect to server'.
